---
title: "vascr - tools for processing vascular impedance data"
author: "James Hucklesby"
date: "03/04/2020"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Vascar}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Overview
vascr provides a toolbox for the analyasis of data generated by ECIS, cellZscope and xcelligence systems. Functions are provided for importing data from each instrument, as well as rapidly running common processing tasks and generating graphical and statistical representations of the data.

## Instalation
```{r eval = FALSE}
# The easiest way to install this package is from CRAN
install.packages("vascr")

# Or the developmetnt version is available from GitHub:
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("JamesHucklesby/vascr")


```











## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
